title: Quantum Logic Codes: Complete Transversal Logical Clifford Instruction Sets for High-Rate Stabilizer Quantum Error Correcting Codes
abstract: We study the structure and transversal logical capabilities of stabilizer quantum error correcting codes. Among our results, we identify universal lower bounds on circuit depth to generate a full logical Clifford algebra, and develop novel constructions of logical transversal gates including a new depth-one transversal phase $\mathrm{\overline{S}}$ gate in the rotated surface code and a depth-one intra-block $\mathrm{\overline{CZ}}$ gate in the 2D-toric code that generalizes to all odd distances and all lengths $L\ge3$, respectively. Finally, we construct a high-rate non-LDPC CSS code family with parameters $[[n,\sqrt{n},Θ({n^β})]]$ where $β\approx 0.2823$ in one demonstrated case, that provably possesses a constant-depth complete 2-local transversal logical Clifford basis instruction set architecture (ISA) composed of all individually targeted $\mathrm{\overline{S}}$, $\mathrm{\overline{SHS}} = \sqrt{X}$, and $\mathrm{\overline{CZ}}$ gates. This ISA is depth-one for certain subfamilies that we design and generally constant-depth under certain conditions. The code family is built from a small code with parameters $[[n_0, 2, d_0]]$, and is tunable in the standard way: it tiles out to form utility-scale logical qubit counts, and it scales up through concatenation to achieve higher distances and error suppression. We show that this construction preserves the depth-one complete transversal logical Clifford basis ISA when composed with these commuting construction actions, inheriting structure from the core codes so that at scale the complete logical Clifford basis ISA remains depth-one up to depth-two addressable operations between tiled cores. We call these Quantum Logic Codes.
summary: This theoretical work develops a deep understanding of how local transversal gates can generate universal logical operations on quantum error correcting codes. By constructing 'Quantum Logic Codes,' the authors demonstrate methods to achieve a complete, low-depth instruction set architecture necessary for scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computation.
main_problem: The study aims to characterize the structure and determine lower bounds on the circuit depth required to implement a full logical Clifford group using local transversal gates within stabilizer quantum error correcting codes.
main_result: The authors constructed 'Quantum Logic Codes,' a high-rate non-LDPC CSS code family possessing a constant-depth complete 2-local transversal logical Clifford basis instruction set architecture (ISA).
method: The work uses symplectic geometry and algebraic constraints derived from analyzing how local physical gates act on the stabilizer group structure to establish bounds on achievable logical operations.
model_or_system: The model is based on CSS stabilizer quantum error correcting codes, which encode physical qubits into blocks. The analysis focuses on realizing fault-tolerant logic by generating the full logical Clifford algebra using transversal gates.
key_observables: Circuit Depth (D), Logical Pauli Operators (S, CZ, etc.), and the structure of the reachable set within $	ext{Sp}(2K, \mathbb{F}_2)$.
important_parameters: Code parameters $[[n, k, d]]$, locality $W$ (1-local, 2-local), and asymptotic scaling exponents like $ eta \approx 0.2823$.
main_assumptions: The analysis heavily relies on the 'confinement hypothesis' regarding the logical action of local gates, and approximations are made when treating W-local operations as depth-one.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate single-layer transversal gate layers (W-local), specific constructions like the Rotated Surface Code S gate and Toric Code CZ gate, and tables detail the required depth for various logical Clifford basis generators on different code families.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing general lower bounds on circuit depth based on code parameters. It then analyzes the algebraic structure of 1-local and 2-local gates using symplectic forms. Finally, it presents specific constructions achieving constant or low polynomial depth for a complete logical Clifford ISA.
why_interesting: The rigorous mathematical framework connecting physical hardware constraints (locality $W$) to abstract computational power (full Clifford group) is highly relevant for designing practical fault-tolerant quantum computers.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Both layers have row-0 CZ pairs h(0, c) ↔v(0, c) combined with row-0 S gates on the h(0, c) qubits, two cross-pair families per tier r = 1, . . . , ⌊(L −1)/2⌋joining rows a = r and b = L −r, and inner-row S† gates on row s = ⌈L/2⌉. Total weights w(3) = 15 and w(5) = 35; the long diagonals visible in the L = 5 panel correspond to cross pairs wrapping across the torus boundary.

FIG. 4. Construction intuition for the Quantum Logic Codes (schematic; the literal transversal gate layers are not shown). A small core code (left) carries a complete logical-Clifford basis ISA at low circuit depth. Two composition operations grow the code parameters while preserving that ISA: tiling (×r, top) places r copies side by side, raising the logical-qubit count to 2r at fixed distance and supplying inter-copy addressable CZ; concatenation with the Steane [[7, 1, 3]] code (×ℓ, bottom) replaces each physical qubit by an inner block, tripling the distance per level at fixed k.
